question: What is the one parameter for RSVP path selection that is included in the 'path' specification?
Which of the following are parameters that can be used for Traffic Engineering (TE)?
What mechanism (or object) does RSVP use to communicate the explicit path to downstream routers?
What parameter must be specified or configured for Traffic Engineering (TE) parameters to actually be used in determining the LSP path?
Based on the reference topology, why would a path specification with 'hop 10 10.10.10.4 strict' and 'hop 20 10.10.10.6 strict' fail for an LSP from R1 to R6?
options: Missing R2 or R3 as an intermediate hop
correct_answers: Missing R2 or R3 as an intermediate hop

question: Match the following troubleshooting tools to their characteristics.
options: Uni-directional test at the LSP level
Uni-directional test at the SDP level
Bi-directional test at the service level

correct_answers.A: Uni-directional test at the LSP level
correct_answers.B: Uni-directional test at the SDP level
correct_answers.C: Bi-directional test at the service level

question: What is the specific MPLS-related 'trace' command used to help understand LSP paths and unanticipated changes?
By what mechanism are Traffic Engineering (TE) properties propagated through an OSPF network?
What is a restriction on the propagation of Traffic Engineering (TE) properties in OSPF?
options: They propagate across the entire AS
They only propagate to the ABR
They are limited to the backbone area (Area 0)

question: Which of these routing protocols is capable of carrying Traffic Engineering (TE) properties?
What default mechanism does RSVP use as a 'keep-alive' for the LSPs it creates?
In the context of this course, what does the acronym 'TED' stand for?
Match the LSP path description sections to their definitions.
options: Determined by the CSPF algorithm taking TE constraints into account
Obtained from the RSVP RRO; resulting path currently active

correct_answers.B: Determined by the CSPF algorithm taking TE constraints into account
correct_answers.C: Obtained from the RSVP RRO; resulting path currently active

question: Which of the following are recognized uses for MPLS as discussed in the course materials?
In the SR OS, what is the maximum number of link 'colours' (admin groups) that may exist?
What is the maximum number of link 'colours' that may be applied to a single link in the SR OS?
For Type 10 LSAs, what are the two top-level TLV sub-types they contain?
When defining a distributed ePipe service, which item is considered globally significant?
What is the primary difference between a local ePipe service and a distributed ePipe service?
options: Local requires BGP, distributed uses OSPF
Local won't have/need an SDP, distributed uses exactly one SDP
Local uses tags, distributed is untagged
Local is for voice, distributed is for data
correct_answers: Local won't have/need an SDP, distributed uses exactly one SDP

question: In the context of SAP encapsulation, what does 'Null encapsulation' specifically mean?
options: No Q-tags (Dot1q or QinQ) are used
The VLAN number is set to 4095
correct_answers: No Q-tags (Dot1q or QinQ) are used
